from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:

    predicate: str

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about an athlete (or related entities) into a fluent sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping from predicate to a lambda that builds a phrase.
    # The lambda receives (subject, object) and returns a string.
    templates = {
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born on {o}",
        "birthYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "height": lambda s, o: f"{s} is {o} meters tall",
        "weight": lambda s, o: f"{s} weighs {o}",
        "position": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays as a {o}",
        "club": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays for {o}",
        "currentclub": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays for {o}",
        "formerTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} formerly played for {o}",
        "league": lambda s, o: f"{s} competes in the {o}",
        "manager": lambda s, o: f"{s} is managed by {o}",
        "coach": lambda s, o: f"{s} is coached by {o}",
        "draftYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was drafted in {o}",
        "draftRound": lambda s, o: f"{s} was selected in round {o}",
        "draftPick": lambda s, o: f"{s} was the {o}th pick",
        "playerNumber": lambda s, o: f"{s} wears number {o}",
        "currentteam": lambda s, o: f"{s} currently plays for {o}",
        "debutTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} debuted with {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"The leader of {s} is {o}",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} died in {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {o}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in {o}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"{s} is located in {o}",
        "ground": lambda s, o: f"The home ground of {s} is {o}",
        "stadium": lambda s, o: f"The stadium of {s} is {o}",
        "owner": lambda s, o: f"{s} is owned by {o}",
        "generalManager": lambda s, o: f"The general manager of {s} is {o}",
        "chairman": lambda s, o: f"The chairman of {s} is {o}",
        "season": lambda s, o: f"{s} participated in the {o} season",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"The currency of {s} is {o}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"The demonym for {s} is {o}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"The official language of {s} is {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"{o} is spoken in {s}",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"{s} includes the ethnic group {o}",
        "anthem": lambda s, o: f"{o} is the anthem of {s}",
        "mostChampions": lambda s, o: f"The team with the most championships in {s} is {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"The leader title of {s} is {o}",
        "utcOffset": lambda s, o: f"The UTC offset for {s} is {o}",
        "timeZone": lambda s, o: f"The time zone of {s} is {o}",
        "areaTotal": lambda s, o: f"The total area of {s} is {o}",
        "foundingDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded on {o}",
    }

    clauses = []
    for t in triples:
        s, p, o = t.subject, t.predicate, t.object
        phrase_func = templates.get(p)
        if phrase_func:
            phrase = phrase_func(s, o)
        else:
            # Generic fallback: use a natural sounding pattern.
            phrase = f"The {p} of {s} is {o}"
        clauses.append(phrase)

    # Combine clauses into a single fluent sentence.
    if not clauses:
        return ""
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        return clauses[0] + "."
    # Join with commas and an 'and' before the last clause.
    combined = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."
    return combined

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about an athlete (or related entities) into a fluent, single sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to turn a numeric string (possibly quoted) into an ordinal word/number.
    def ordinal(num_str: str) -> str:
        num = int(num_str.strip('\"').strip())
        suffix = "th"
        if 10 <= num % 100 <= 20:
            suffix = "th"
        else:
            if num % 10 == 1:
                suffix = "st"
            elif num % 10 == 2:
                suffix = "nd"
            elif num % 10 == 3:
                suffix = "rd"
        return f"{num}{suffix}"

    # Group triples by subject to allow merging of related information.
    subj_map: dict[str, list[tuple[str, str]]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        subj_map.setdefault(t.subject, []).append((t.predicate, t.object))

    sentences: list[str] = []

    for subj, props in subj_map.items():
        # Separate predicates for special handling.
        clubs = []
        currentclubs = []
        former_teams = []
        other_phrases = []

        for pred, obj in props:
            # Clean object from surrounding quotes if any.
            obj_clean = obj.strip('\"')
            if pred == "club":
                clubs.append(obj_clean)
            elif pred == "currentclub":
                currentclubs.append(obj_clean)
            elif pred == "formerTeam":
                former_teams.append(obj_clean)
            elif pred == "draftPick":
                other_phrases.append(f"{subj} was pick number {ordinal(obj_clean)}")
            elif pred == "playerNumber":
                other_phrases.append(f"{subj} wears number {obj_clean}")
            elif pred == "birthDate":
                other_phrases.append(f"{subj} was born on {obj_clean}")
            elif pred == "birthYear":
                other_phrases.append(f"{subj} was born in {obj_clean}")
            elif pred == "birthPlace":
                other_phrases.append(f"{subj} was born in {obj_clean}")
            elif pred == "height":
                other_phrases.append(f"{subj} is {obj_clean} meters tall")
            elif pred == "weight":
                other_phrases.append(f"{subj} weighs {obj_clean}")
            elif pred == "position":
                other_phrases.append(f"{subj} plays as a {obj_clean}")
            elif pred == "league":
                other_phrases.append(f"{subj} competes in the {obj_clean}")
            elif pred == "manager":
                other_phrases.append(f"{subj} is managed by {obj_clean}")
            elif pred == "coach":
                other_phrases.append(f"{subj} is coached by {obj_clean}")
            elif pred == "draftYear":
                other_phrases.append(f"{subj} was drafted in {obj_clean}")
            elif pred == "draftRound":
                other_phrases.append(f"{subj} was selected in round {obj_clean}")
            elif pred == "currentteam":
                other_phrases.append(f"{subj} currently plays for {obj_clean}")
            elif pred == "debutTeam":
                other_phrases.append(f"{subj} debuted with {obj_clean}")
            elif pred == "city":
                other_phrases.append(f"{subj} is based in {obj_clean}")
            elif pred == "ground":
                other_phrases.append(f"The home ground of {subj} is {obj_clean}")
            elif pred == "stadium":
                other_phrases.append(f"The stadium of {subj} is {obj_clean}")
            elif pred == "owner":
                other_phrases.append(f"{subj} is owned by {obj_clean}")
            elif pred == "generalManager":
                other_phrases.append(f"The general manager of {subj} is {obj_clean}")
            elif pred == "chairman":
                other_phrases.append(f"The chairman of {subj} is {obj_clean}")
            elif pred == "season":
                other_phrases.append(f"{subj} participated in the {obj_clean} season")
            elif pred == "currency":
                other_phrases.append(f"The currency of {subj} is {obj_clean}")
            elif pred == "demonym":
                other_phrases.append(f"The demonym for {subj} is {obj_clean}")
            elif pred == "officialLanguage":
                other_phrases.append(f"The official language of {subj} is {obj_clean}")
            elif pred == "language":
                other_phrases.append(f"{obj_clean} is spoken in {subj}")
            elif pred == "ethnicGroup":
                other_phrases.append(f"{subj} includes the ethnic group {obj_clean}")
            elif pred == "anthem":
                other_phrases.append(f"{obj_clean} is the anthem of {subj}")
            elif pred == "mostChampions":
                other_phrases.append(f"The team with the most championships in {subj} is {obj_clean}")
            elif pred == "leaderTitle":
                other_phrases.append(f"The leader title of {subj} is {obj_clean}")
            elif pred == "utcOffset":
                other_phrases.append(f"The UTC offset for {subj} is {obj_clean}")
            elif pred == "timeZone":
                other_phrases.append(f"The time zone of {subj} is {obj_clean}")
            elif pred == "areaTotal":
                other_phrases.append(f"The total area of {subj} is {obj_clean}")
            elif pred == "foundingDate":
                other_phrases.append(f"{subj} was founded on {obj_clean}")
            else:
                other_phrases.append(f"The {pred} of {subj} is {obj_clean}")

        # Build club related phrase.
        club_phrase = ""
        if former_teams:
            ft = ", ".join(former_teams)
            club_phrase += f"{subj}'s former team{' was' if len(former_teams)==1 else 's were'} {ft}. "
        if currentclubs:
            cc = ", ".join(currentclubs)
            club_phrase += f"{subj} currently plays for {cc}. "
        if clubs:
            cl = ", ".join(clubs)
            club_phrase += f"{subj} plays for {cl}. "

        # Combine all parts for this subject.
        parts = [club_phrase.strip()] if club_phrase else []
        parts.extend(other_phrases)

        # Join with proper punctuation.
        subject_sentence = " ".join(parts).strip()
        if subject_sentence:
            sentences.append(subject_sentence)

    # Join different subject sentences with a space.
    final_text = " ".join(sentences)
    # Ensure final punctuation.
    if not final_text.endswith('.'):
        final_text += '.'
    return final_text
